results.title: Troubleshoot recording issues in meetings - Google Meet Help
results.content:
Google Meet Help

Sign in

Google Help

 Help Center

+ Get started with Google Meet+ Start or join a video meeting+ Gemini in Meet+ During the meeting+ After the meeting+ Feature availability+ Settings in Google Meet+ Calling in Google Meet+ Troubleshooting
 Community Google Meet

 Privacy Policy Terms of Service Submit feedback

Send feedback on...

 Help Center
 Community

Google Meet

# Troubleshoot recording issues in meetings

Important:

 The ability to record a meeting is only available on a computer.
 Recording video meetings is only available for meetings organized by certain Google Workspace editions.
 Participants can only record a meeting if the host has permission to record.

Here’s how to troubleshoot issues when you record a meeting.

Learn how to record a video meeting. [...] Confirm that your admin has turned on recording for Meet in the Google Admin console.
 Verify that you’re using Meet on a computer. Recording is only available for the computer version.
 You can’t record if you join only to present, such as from a laptop while already in a video conference room. Join the video meeting first, start presenting, and then record.
 You can’t record if the meeting was created on a conference room device (such as Meet hardware), or was generated by another process, such as a Chrome plug in. Schedule the meeting in Calendar or be sure a person, and not a device, starts any ad-hoc meetings. [...] Recording may be disabled if:

 Your admin turned off recording.
 Your Workspace edition doesn't support recording.
 Your Google Drive is disabled.
 You aren't allowed to record a specific video meeting.
 You're in a breakout room. Breakout rooms don't support recordings.
 Your admin doesn't allow you to create new files in Drive.
 The meeting organizer doesn't have permission to record. Even if you have the necessary permissions as a participant, you can’t record a meeting if the host doesn't have them.

Check with your admin to make sure:

 Meet supports recording for you and your organization.
 Recording is on for video meetings.

Can't find the recording button

results.title: Problems with Meeting Notes and How to Solve Them
results.content:
At Transcription City LTD, we’ve been supporting organisations with professional note-taking and minute-taking since 2011. Over the years, we’ve seen first-hand the frustrations companies face when they try to manage this work internally or rely solely on automated tools. The good news is that every problem has a solution and we’re here to share both empathy and expertise.

## 1. Privacy and Compliance Concerns

The problem: Many professionals worry about GDPR breaches, data leaks, or unapproved recordings. These concerns are not trivial; a single mistake can damage trust and expose a company to legal risk.  
 Why it matters: Meeting minutes often include sensitive details about staff, finances, or strategy. When handled carelessly, those details can fall into the wrong hands. [...] Our solution: Accuracy is the foundation of our service. Our experienced team listens carefully and produces minutes that are clear, reliable, and easy to follow. Each set of notes goes through a quality check, so you don’t have to second-guess the details.

## 3. Missed Content

The problem: Bots fail to join, recordings cut out, or someone forgets to hit “record.” Once information is lost, it’s gone forever. That missing detail might be a critical decision or an action point that changes the direction of a project.

Our solution: We attend live — either in person or remotely — and always have backup systems in place. Because of this, we capture every point, every time. You don’t need to worry about technology letting you down.

## 4. Overly Long Notes [...] Our solution: We don’t just promise security we prove it. Transcription City LTD is certified to ISO 27001, ISO 9001, and Cyber Essentials Plus. We are registered data controllers and operate with GDPR compliance built into every process. That means every piece of information stays safe and confidential.

## 2. Inaccurate Records

The problem: Automated note-taking tools frequently struggle with jargon, multiple speakers, or different accents. Even humans without training often capture too much fluff and miss the key outcomes. This leads to confusion, wasted time, and disputes about what was actually agreed.

      Dear @Aaron Pook,

      I hope this message finds you well!

      I just wanted to check in and see if you have any updates or additional information you’d like to share regarding your situation. If there's anything new or if you need further assistance, please don’t hesitate to let me know.

      Looking forward to your response!

     Sign in to comment
2. AI answer

    2026-04-28T23:00:00.8733333+00:00

   The meeting recording itself is stored and processed outside the Teams client, so when only a short, blank segment is available in OneDrive it usually indicates a recording/processing failure rather than an issue with the local app. [...] If the recording still only shows a duration of 20 minutes, the most appropriate next step for further troubleshooting would be to report the issue to the relevant team. I sincerely recommend you try to create a support ticket from Microsoft 365 Admin Center>Support>New Service Request. You can raise support ticket from: 

   The support team over there has higher permission than us to investigate the issue from background, they have more resource and correct escalation channel in handling this kind of issues. This would be a more efficient way in handling the case for you.

   For reference:  Get support | Microsoft Docs

results.title: Issues that affect meeting recordings - Microsoft Teams | Microsoft Learn
results.content:
### Issue 1: The Meeting recording button is missing

 Leave and rejoin the meeting. This might restore the recording functionality.
 Use the Teams web client to join and record the meeting.
 Make sure that the user is trying to record a meeting, not a 1:1 call. Call recording is controlled by the `AllowCloudRecordingForCalls` parameter of Teams calling policies.
 Run the 1:1 Call Recording Diagnostic for an affected user:

  1. Sign in to Microsoft 365 admin center, and type Diag: Teams 1:1 Call Recording in the Need Help? search box.
  2. Enter the email address of the affected user in the Username or Email field, and then select Run Tests.

Try again to record the meeting. If the issue still isn't resolved, open a service request for Microsoft Support. [...] To determine whether these issues are caused by the user account, run the Teams Meeting Recording Test. This diagnostic checks whether the account meets all requirements to record a meeting in Teams. If the issue persists, try the following troubleshooting methods.

If you see a Lock icon on the recording button, hover over the icon to see a tooltip that explains why the button is unavailable.

Administrators can run the Meeting Recording Support Diagnostic that's available in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

The diagnostic can check the following prerequisites for Teams meeting recordings:

 Users must be assigned the correct license.
 Users must have the correct meeting policies. [...] The tests will suggest next steps to address any tenant or policy configurations in order to validate that the user is correctly configured to record a meeting in Teams.

## Workaround

Begin by making sure that the user has the latest Teams updates installed.

If the issue isn't resolved by an update, review the following issues, and use the workaround for the appropriate issue.

### Issue 1: The Meeting recording button is missing
